preview_text:
The model assesses Croatia as a heavy favorite with a 79% win probability, compared to Ghana's modest 3% chance. This 76 percentage point gap reflects a sharp imbalance in the forecast. The underdog's path to victory is narrow under the statistical model's estimates.

The outcome distribution reveals a contest tilted decisively toward one side. A Croatian victory is the dominant scenario by a wide margin. A draw registers as plausible at 18%, offering Ghana a secondary route to avoid defeat, though the probability of a Ghana win remains marginal. The match is not expected to be closely competitive on the model's view.

The model and market diverge materially here. Sportsbooks assign Croatia 57.5% and Ghana 17.3%, placing the underdog odds more than 14 percentage points higher than the model estimates and draw probability 7 points lower. Polymarket prices fall between these two, closer to the sportsbook consensus. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better captures the true likelihood, only that a meaningful gap exists.
